When paying with QR codes, check the merchant's name and amount carefully, it could be a fake QR code.
Keep your bank account information safe, don't give it to anyone, to prevent unauthorized transactions.
If someone asks for money saying you've won a big prize, don't give it, that could be a social engineering trap.
Update your mobile banking app regularly, it strengthens security and protects against unauthorized transactions.
If you see any suspicious person around you while withdrawing money from an ATM, immediately inform the police, it could be ATM skimming.
Someone might misuse your photos or videos on social media, so keep your profile secure, to avoid identity theft.
Read SMS or emails from the bank carefully, if you have any doubts, contact the bank immediately, it could be phishing.
Don't give your bank account details to anyone, even relatives, there might be unauthorized transactions.
If someone asks for money promising to arrange a loan easily, don't give it, they might be a fake agent.
If you see any suspicious object in the card slot of the ATM, immediately inform the bank, it could be an ATM skimming attempt.
Don't give your OTP number to anyone, even if they are a bank employee, avoid OTP fraud.
If someone calls claiming to be from the bank and asks for your ATM card number and PIN code, hang up immediately, it could be a phishing call.
When scanning a QR code, check the information displayed by the scanner carefully, it could be a fake QR code.
If you see any suspicious transactions in your bank account statement, contact the bank immediately, there might be unauthorized transactions.
If you let someone use your bank account by showing you greed, you may get into legal trouble, be careful, it could be identity theft.
If someone asks you for personal information on social media, don't give it, that could be a social engineering attempt.
Keep your mobile banking app safe, don't let anyone use it, and prevent unauthorized transactions.
When withdrawing money from an ATM, cover the keyboard when entering your code and only leave the ATM after taking your card, avoid ATM skimming.
If someone calls you and tries to scare you into withdrawing money from your account, contact your bank immediately, it could be phishing.
